content.problemStatement:
Currently, it is X:00X:00X:00 hours on December 31st31^{st}31st and you are wondering how many hours are left till midnight.

For the purposes of this problem, we use a 242424 hour system. So, XXX can range from 000 to 232323, and you need to tell the number of hours left till 00:0000:0000:00 of the next day.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of hours left till midnight
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (0 ≤ X ≤ 23) representing the current hour on December 31st in a 24‑hour clock.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the number of whole hours left until midnight of the next day.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: X = 0 → 24 - 0 = 24 hours left. Second test case: X = 23 → 24 - 23 = 1 hour left.
X = 12 → 12 hours left. X = 5 → 19 hours left. X = 15 → 9 hours left.

solution.approach: The day has exactly 24 hours. If the current hour is X, the remaining whole hours until the next midnight are simply 24 - X. This works for all allowed X because when X = 0 the answer is 24 (a full day left) and when X = 23 the answer is 1. The algorithm processes each test case independently, performing one subtraction per case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
